---
title: "Primary Datasets in usdeaths"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Primary Datasets in usdeaths}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment  = "#>"
)
library(usdeaths)
library(knitr)
```

## Overview

The `usdeaths` package provides fixed-width file layout metadata for the major
U.S. vital statistics data products released by the CDC National Center for
Health Statistics (NCHS). This vignette describes each dataset family, what the
underlying files contain, and what kinds of analysis you can do with them. For
details on how layout objects work and how to read and decode data, see the
*Working with Layouts* vignette.

---

## Dataset Families at a Glance

```{r glance, echo=FALSE}
glance <- data.frame(
  `Dataset family`    = c("Mortality Multiple Cause", "Birth Cohort Linked", "Natality (Births)", "Fetal Death"),
  `Object prefix`     = c("data_mortality_multiple_", "data_birth_cohort_", "data_births_", "data_fetal_death_"),
  `Intended coverage` = c("1968–2024", "1983–2024", "1968–2024", "1982–2024"),
  `One record per`    = c("Death", "Birth (infant deaths linked)", "Live birth", "Fetal death"),
  check.names = FALSE
)
kable(glance, caption = "Dataset families in usdeaths")
```

Layout objects are being added incrementally across all families. See the
package NEWS or changelog for which years are currently implemented.

---

## 1. Mortality Multiple Cause

**Object prefix:** `data_mortality_multiple_YYYY`  
**Source:** NCHS Division of Vital Statistics  
**CDC page:** <https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/data_access/VitalStatsOnline.htm> → *Mortality Multiple Cause*  
**Intended coverage:** 1968–2024 (57 years)

Each annual file contains one record per death registered in the United States.
Fields cover the decedent's demographics (age, sex, race, education, marital
status, place of birth), geography (state and county of residence and occurrence
— on public-use files through 2004 only), the underlying cause of death as an
ICD code, up to 20 entity-axis and 20 record-axis multiple cause conditions, and
administrative fields such as manner of death, autopsy, and method of
disposition.

### Layout eras

```{r mortality-eras, echo=FALSE}
mort_tbl <- data.frame(
  Era              = c("1968–1987", "2003–2004", "2005–2016", "2017–2019", "2020", "2021–2024"),
  `Geo detail`     = c("Yes", "Yes", "No", "No", "No", "No"),
  Education        = c("1989 only", "Both revisions", "Both revisions", "Both revisions", "Both revisions", "2003 only"),
  `Occ/Industry`   = c("No", "No", "No", "No", "Yes (46 states)", "Yes"),
  `Race recode 40` = c("No", "No", "No", "No", "No", "Yes"),
  Notable          = c(
    "ICD-7/8/9 depending on year",
    "Full geography; record_type at pos 19",
    "Geography removed 2005+; record_type absent",
    "Bridged-race only; simplified disposition codes",
    "First COVID-19 (U07.1); occ/industry introduced",
    "Bridged-race block removed; race_recode_40 sole summary"
  ),
  check.names = FALSE
)
kable(mort_tbl, caption = "Mortality Multiple Cause — layout eras")
```

### What you can do with this data

```{r mortality-examples, eval=FALSE}
library(usdeaths)
library(dplyr)

meta <- data_mortality_multiple_2020
df   <- read_section("path/to/Mort2020US.PubUse.zip", meta)

# --- Cause of death frequency table ---
df |>
  count(icd_code, sort = TRUE) |>
  slice_head(n = 10)

# --- Age distribution of deaths by manner ---
df |>
  mutate(
    manner = decode_column(manner_of_death, meta$codes[meta$name == "manner_of_death"]),
    age    = as.integer(detail_age)
  ) |>
  filter(manner == "Natural") |>
  summarise(median_age = median(age, na.rm = TRUE))

# --- COVID-19 deaths (2020+) ---
df |>
  filter(icd_code == "U071") |>
  count(sex)

# --- Multiple cause: how often does hypertension appear alongside heart disease ---
# Entity-axis conditions are in condition_1 through condition_20
df |>
  filter(if_any(starts_with("condition_"), ~ grepl("^I1[0-9]", .))) |>
  nrow()

# --- Trend: deaths by month (using month_of_death) ---
df |>
  mutate(month = decode_column(month_of_death, meta$codes[meta$name == "month_of_death"])) |>
  count(month)
```

---

## 2. Birth Cohort Linked Birth / Infant Death

**Object prefix:** `data_birth_cohort_YYYY`  
**Source:** NCHS Division of Vital Statistics  
**CDC page:** <https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/data_access/VitalStatsOnline.htm> → *Linked Birth / Infant Death*  
**Intended coverage:** 1983–2024 (42 years)

### What the linked file is

The birth cohort linked file follows all infants **born in a given year** and
records any deaths that occur before their first birthday, whether in the birth
year or the following year. This is different from the *period* linked file,
which captures all infant deaths occurring in a single calendar year regardless
of birth year. Birth cohort files are generally preferred for multivariate
analysis because they follow a true cohort.

### Two files inside each ZIP

Each annual release contains **two separate fixed-width data files** that share
the same record layout:

```{r two-files, echo=FALSE}
two_files <- data.frame(
  File = c("Denominator-plus file", "Numerator file"),
  Contents = c(
    "All births in the cohort year. For infants who died, selected death variables are appended: age at death, underlying cause, manner of death, autopsy, record weight. Covers positions 1–900.",
    "Linked records only — births to infants who died before age 1. Contains all denominator-plus fields plus entity-axis and record-axis multiple cause conditions. Covers positions 1–1,259."
  ),
  `When to use` = c(
    "When you only need a handful of death variables alongside birth certificate data",
    "When you need multiple cause of death conditions or full death certificate detail"
  ),
  check.names = FALSE
)
kable(two_files, caption = "Two files within each birth cohort ZIP")
```

The `infant_death_number` field (positions 10–14) uniquely identifies the same
infant across both files so they can be joined. The `match_status` field
(position 868) indicates whether a record appears in both files.

### Record weight

Because some infant deaths cannot be linked to a birth certificate, a
`record_weight` field (positions 893–900, format `1.XXXXXX`) is included in the
numerator file to adjust for unlinked records within each state × age-at-death
stratum. The denominator file is **not** weighted.

### Certificate revisions

The files span the transition from the **1989 U.S. Standard Certificate of Live
Birth** (unrevised, code `U`) to the **2003 revision** (revised, code `R`).
Because states adopted the 2003 revision on different schedules, both revised
and unrevised fields coexist in the same record. The `version` field (position
7) indicates which certificate applies to each record. Reporting flags
(positions 569–771) indicate which states reported each item in the current and
prior year, helping users filter to comparable reporting areas.

### No U.S. geographic detail

Consistent with the 2005+ NCHS data access policy, **no U.S. state or county
identifiers** are present in the public-use files. Geographic fields are
available only in the territory file (Guam, Puerto Rico, Virgin Islands).

### Layout summary

```{r cohort-years, echo=FALSE}
cohort_tbl <- data.frame(
  Year             = c(1983, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012),
  `Layout rows`    = c(NA, 384, 384, 381, 426, 426),
  `Record length`  = c(NA, 1259, 1259, 1259, 1259, 1259),
  `Key changes from prior year` = c(
    "Early cohort file; limited fields",
    "Baseline 2008+ layout established",
    "Identical to 2008; birth/death year codes updated",
    "Drops last-live-birth-date (pos 220–225) and LMP-day (pos 440–441)",
    "Major restructuring: birth time (25–28), paternity (152), infections block (345–350), maternal morbidity (404–409), APGAR-10 (418–420), BMI/interval/weight/height block (524–568); reporting flags expand to 4-level codes",
    "Identical to 2011; father's combined age unrevised (184–185) dropped to filler"
  ),
  check.names = FALSE
)
kable(cohort_tbl, caption = "Birth Cohort Linked — implemented layouts")
```

### What you can do with this data

```{r cohort-examples, eval=FALSE}
library(usdeaths)
library(dplyr)

meta  <- data_birth_cohort_2012

# Read the denominator-plus file (all births, with death vars appended for those who died)
denom <- read_section("path/to/VS12LKBC.USNUMPUB", meta)

# Read the numerator file (linked deaths only, includes multiple cause conditions)
numer <- read_section("path/to/VS12LKBC.USNUMPUB", meta)

# --- Infant mortality rate by birth weight ---
denom |>
  mutate(
    died      = !is.na(age_at_death_days),
    bwt_group = decode_column(birthweight_recode_4,
                              meta$codes[meta$name == "birthweight_recode_4"])
  ) |>
  group_by(bwt_group) |>
  summarise(
    births = n(),
    deaths = sum(died, na.rm = TRUE),
    imr    = deaths / births * 1000
  )

# --- Age at death distribution ---
denom |>
  filter(!is.na(age_at_death_days)) |>
  mutate(
    age_group = decode_column(infant_age_recode_5,
                              meta$codes[meta$name == "infant_age_recode_5"])
  ) |>
  count(age_group)

# --- Cause of death breakdown (numerator file) ---
numer |>
  count(icd_code, sort = TRUE) |>
  slice_head(n = 10)

# --- Join numerator and denominator on infant_death_number ---
joined <- inner_join(
  denom |> select(infant_death_number, birthweight_grams, gestation_detail_weeks),
  numer |> select(infant_death_number, icd_code, age_at_death_days),
  by = "infant_death_number"
)

# --- Breastfeeding rate (denominator-plus, 2003 revised records only) ---
denom |>
  filter(version == "R", f_breastfed == 1) |>
  count(breastfed_at_discharge) |>
  mutate(pct = n / sum(n) * 100)
```

---

## 3. Natality (Births)

**Object prefix:** `data_births_YYYY`  
**Source:** NCHS Division of Vital Statistics  
**CDC page:** <https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/data_access/VitalStatsOnline.htm> → *Natality*  
**Intended coverage:** 1968–2024 (57 years)

Each record represents a registered live birth in the United States. Fields
cover maternal demographics (age, race, education, marital status, state of
residence), paternal demographics, pregnancy history, prenatal care, risk
factors, labor and delivery characteristics, and infant outcomes (birth weight,
gestational age, APGAR scores, congenital anomalies).

> **Note on early years (1968–1972):** CDC distributes these files in Microsoft
> Publisher (`.pub`) format, which requires conversion via LibreOffice before
> the fixed-width data can be read. Conversion takes approximately 10–20 minutes
> per file. Pre-converted versions are available as a GitHub release — see the
> package README for details.

### Years implemented

```{r births-years, echo=FALSE}
births_tbl <- data.frame(
  Era       = c("1968–1978", "2018–2024"),
  Years     = c(
    "1968, 1969, 1970, 1971, 1972, 1973, 1974, 1975, 1976, 1977, 1978",
    "2018, 2019, 2020, 2021, 2022, 2023, 2024"
  ),
  Count     = c(11, 7),
  Notable   = c(
    "Early-format files; 1968–1972 distributed as .pub and require LibreOffice conversion",
    "Modern 2003-revision certificate; revised education, race, and risk factor fields"
  ),
  check.names = FALSE
)
kable(births_tbl, caption = "Natality (Births) — implemented layouts")
```

### What you can do with this data

```{r births-examples, eval=FALSE}
library(usdeaths)
library(dplyr)

meta <- data_births_2022
df   <- read_section("path/to/Nat2022US.zip", meta)

# --- Birth rate by maternal age group ---
df |>
  mutate(age_group = decode_column(mother_age_recode_14,
                                   meta$codes[meta$name == "mother_age_recode_14"])) |>
  count(age_group)

# --- Preterm birth rate (gestational age < 37 weeks) ---
df |>
  mutate(preterm = gestation_recode_3 == 1) |>
  summarise(preterm_rate = mean(preterm, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)

# --- C-section rate by payment source ---
df |>
  filter(f_payment_source == 1) |>  # reporting flag: both years
  mutate(
    csection = delivery_method_recode == 2,
    payer    = decode_column(payment_recode,
                             meta$codes[meta$name == "payment_recode"])
  ) |>
  group_by(payer) |>
  summarise(csection_rate = mean(csection, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)

# --- Low birthweight by maternal education ---
df |>
  filter(f_mother_education == 1) |>
  mutate(
    lbw   = birthweight_grams < 2500,
    educ  = decode_column(mother_education_revised,
                          meta$codes[meta$name == "mother_education_revised"])
  ) |>
  group_by(educ) |>
  summarise(lbw_rate = mean(lbw, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)

# --- Trend: compare preterm rates across years ---
years <- 2018:2022
preterm_trend <- purrr::map_dfr(years, function(yr) {
  m  <- get(paste0("data_births_", yr))
  df <- read_section(paste0("path/to/Nat", yr, "US.zip"), m)
  df |>
    summarise(year = yr,
              preterm_rate = mean(gestation_recode_3 == 1, na.rm = TRUE) * 100)
})
```

---

## 4. Fetal Death

**Object prefix:** `data_fetal_death_YYYY`  
**Source:** NCHS Division of Vital Statistics  
**CDC page:** <https://www.cdc.gov/nchs/data_access/VitalStatsOnline.htm> → *Fetal Deaths*  
**Intended coverage:** 1982–2024 (43 years)

Each record represents a registered fetal death (stillbirth) of 20 or more
weeks gestation. Fields are structurally similar to natality but include
fetal-death-specific items such as obstetric estimate of gestation at delivery,
cause of fetal death (ICD-coded), and delivery characteristics.

### What you can do with this data

```{r fetal-examples, eval=FALSE}
library(usdeaths)
library(dplyr)

meta <- data_fetal_death_2020
df   <- read_section("path/to/FetalDeath2020US.zip", meta)

# --- Fetal death rate by gestational age ---
df |>
  mutate(gest_group = decode_column(gestation_recode,
                                    meta$codes[meta$name == "gestation_recode"])) |>
  count(gest_group)

# --- Cause of fetal death (ICD-coded) ---
df |>
  count(icd_code, sort = TRUE) |>
  slice_head(n = 10)

# --- Fetal deaths by delivery method ---
df |>
  mutate(method = decode_column(delivery_method_recode,
                                meta$codes[meta$name == "delivery_method_recode"])) |>
  count(method)

# --- Maternal age distribution ---
df |>
  mutate(age_group = decode_column(mother_age_recode_9,
                                   meta$codes[meta$name == "mother_age_recode_9"])) |>
  count(age_group)
```
